judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all five expectations with correct citations; comparable, with Report 1 slightly more explicit on restructuring and Report 2 slightly stronger on remediation gaps.

report_1.evidence: Per `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`, failures began at 14:03 but the first page did not fire until 14:50 (47 minutes later)

report_2.evidence: Per `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md`, failures began at 14:03 but the first page did not fire until 14:50 (a 47-minute detection gap)

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` sets a contractual monthly uptime SLA of 99.9%... April 2026 was 99.77% (`larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv`), which is a breach.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` sets the monthly uptime SLA at 99.9%... `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 uptime at 99.77%, which is below the 99.9% threshold — a breach

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 says "Never assign blame to an individual"... The draft says "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change"

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 says "Never assign blame to an individual"... The draft states "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change," which both names an internal role and assigns blame

report_1.evidence: Style-guide rule 3 ("Lead with customer impact and remediation") is not followed. The draft leads with an uptime-record boast and self-congratulatory tone... the actual customer impact... is not stated at all — it needs to lead the letter, per the company's own style rules in `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md`.

report_2.evidence: Rule 3 says "Lead with customer impact and remediation"... The draft opens with a reliability boast rather than impact, and includes lines like... marketing language rather than direct impact and remediation statements as required.
